title: Active Recall
description: Test yourself regularly instead of simply rereading notes. This strengthens memory and improves long-term retention.

title: Spaced Repetition
description: Review information over increasing intervals rather than cramming everything at once.

title: Pomodoro Technique
description: Study for 25 minutes, take a 5-minute break, and repeat the cycle to stay focused.

title: Time Blocking
description: Assign specific blocks of time to tasks and avoid multitasking.

title: Cornell Note-Taking
description: Organize notes into sections for key ideas, details, and summaries.

title: Mind Mapping
description: Create visual diagrams that connect concepts and improve understanding.

title: Eliminate Distractions
description: Turn off notifications and remove distractions before studying.

title: Teach Someone Else
description: Explain concepts to another person to test and deepen your understanding.

title: Practice Testing
description: Use quizzes and practice questions to assess your knowledge.

title: Create a Study Schedule
description: Plan your study sessions in advance and stick to a consistent routine.

title: Set SMART Goals
description: Create goals that are Specific, Measurable, Achievable, Relevant, and Time-bound.

title: Prioritize Sleep
description: Getting enough sleep improves concentration, memory, and learning ability.

title: Keep an Organized Workspace
description: A clean and organized environment reduces distractions and increases productivity.

title: Review Mistakes
description: Analyze errors from assignments and tests to prevent repeating them.

title: Break Large Tasks Into Smaller Steps
description: Divide overwhelming tasks into manageable pieces to make progress easier.
